steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: The line passes through $(9,4)$ and has slope $-\frac{1}{3}$. Use slope-intercept form: $$y=-\frac{1}{3}x+b$$

steps.title: Solve for $b$ and write the equation
steps.content:
Substitute $(9,4)$ to find $b$: $$4=-\frac{1}{3}(9)+b$$
$$4=-3+b$$
$$b=7$$ So the line is $$y=-\frac{x}{3}+7.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The final check is to match the computed result to the answer choices. The value or equation found above matches the correct answer, $y=-\frac{x}{3}+7$. The other choices do not satisfy the same condition from the question, so D is correct.
